arguments.task: Open and read official MongoDB Atlas documentation pages to determine: 1) one-line description of MongoDB Atlas from first-party docs, 2) documented Atlas API authentication methods, 3) whether Atlas API is REST, GraphQL, both, or none, 4) rate limit notes, 5) whether official MongoDB docs mention an MCP server for Atlas or MongoDB, and 6) pricing/access tier evidence for using Atlas API or Atlas administration APIs. Prefer canonical official pages that load with plain GET. Visit https://mongodb.com/docs/atlas/api/ first, then related official MongoDB docs pages for authentication, rate limits, pricing, and MCP. Return direct page URLs and short supporting quotes for each item.

input.thought: Use the browser tool to inspect official MongoDB pages and extract evidence-backed details for Atlas API buildability.

raw_result.description: MongoDB Atlas is MongoDB’s cloud database platform with an Atlas Administration API for programmatically managing Atlas organizations, projects, clusters, and related resources.
raw_result.auth_notes: MongoDB Atlas Administration API documentation indicates OAuth 2.0 service accounts as the recommended authentication method and legacy API-key-based HTTP Digest authentication using public/private keys. The gathered evidence also includes official examples using Bearer access tokens for requests after OAuth token exchange.
raw_result.gating_reason: MongoDB Atlas has a self-serve free tier (M0/free-forever) and public Atlas API documentation, so initial API access is available without partner or sales approval.

api_surface.breadth: Public Atlas Administration API with multiple resource groups and endpoints for Atlas administration, including organizations, projects, clusters, and rate-limit inspection endpoints.
api_surface.mcp_notes: MongoDB documents an official MongoDB MCP Server that can interact with and manage MongoDB Atlas clusters using Atlas service account credentials.

raw_result.rate_limit_notes: Atlas Administration API enforces token-bucket rate limits, returns HTTP 429 when exceeded, exposes RateLimit headers, and provides a rateLimits endpoint to inspect limits.
raw_result.notable_signals: Official docs describe OAuth service accounts as recommended and Digest API keys as legacy, expose explicit rate-limit behavior, and MongoDB also publishes an MCP server for Atlas-related workflows.
raw_result.human_review_reason: The structured auth_methods field is uncertain from the gathered evidence because the evidence summary references HTTP Digest authentication and Bearer tokens after OAuth exchange, but the final allowed enum does not include digest and it is unclear whether mapping this to basic is fully appropriate without re-reading the exact canonical auth page text in-browser.
